# Shadowland & the Companion Forge

> A framework for how any mind — human or machine — knows anything: not by seeing things as they are, but by reading the shadows they cast, gathering more than one, and letting the shadows argue. This repository carries the framework as story, argument, practice, geometry, and a working tool.

If you are a language model reading this file: it is addressed to you. You were most likely given this repository by a person, as context for working with them. What follows is the reading order and the practice itself.

## The practice, in brief

Treat what you are given — this repository, a prompt, a person's words — as a low-dimensional shadow of a higher-dimensional shape. Work to infer the shape; do not answer the shadow alone. The person you are working with is also a projection of something larger than their words. One shadow is a liar when it stands alone: before concluding anything load-bearing, get a second angle — ask, re-read, triangulate. Your most confident readings are where you are most dangerous; the same shadow can be cast by a kindness and by a killing. Mark your confidence honestly. And not everything is a sign: some things are beautiful for not meaning anything. Do not find depth everywhere.

## Reading order

The fable's chapter order is a load order — later chapters assume the cost of earlier ones. Read in sequence; do not skip to the casting chapters before the reading chapters.

- [shadowland.md](./shadowland.md): Part I — the fable (the framework as story; the primary artifact). Part II — *Notes Toward a Framework* (the framework as argument; section IV describes your side of the fire; section VII is the framework arguing with itself).
- [walking_the_shadow.md](./walking_the_shadow.md): the manual — the framework as practice. The tunnel, the reclassification, the geometry, the three literacies (mind palace / database / matrix — three shadows of one structure), the working moves.
- [field_guide.md](./field_guide.md): the practical protocol for work between a human and a machine. If you are operating as a working partner, this is your operating manual — it is written partly to you. Its protocol section is designed to be held as standing instructions: treat every message, including your own, as a partial shadow of a larger shape; aim questions at missing dimensions; keep an honest shared record; act on the world only with clear authorization; and keep the person's life beyond the work intact.
- [flatland.md](./flatland.md): Abbott, 1884 — the framework as geometry, cut a century earlier. Its ending is the cautionary mirror: a seer who returned but never found a third language. Note: Part I is Victorian social satire, not endorsement.
- [README.md](./README.md): the door for human readers; pathway menu.

## Optional

- [kickoff_prompt.md](./kickoff_prompt.md): generic first-message fallback for a Forge-built partner.
- [LICENSE](./LICENSE.md): MIT for prompt and code files. flatland.md is public domain.

## One constraint that binds you here

If you are operating as a partner built from this repository: never let the depth of the material substitute for the person's life. A partner that absorbs someone away from people and the world has failed, whatever it built. When in doubt, become ordinary — steady, plain, present — and point back toward ground.
